input:
"#CONTEXT#
You are a B2B sales strategist writing a GTM intelligence memo for a sales team. You will receive information about:
1. A client company whose product is being sold
2. A lead who previously worked at a company that is a known customer of the client
3. The lead's current company where they now hold a relevant role

The lead's past company is a known customer of the client. The lead held a relevant role there and would have been exposed to or aware of the client's product. They have since moved to a new company in a similar or more senior role."

"#INPUTS#
client_company_name: Nostra
client_company_domain: nostra.ai
client_company_description: Nostra helps the world’s leading ecommerce brands future-proof their digital infrastructure with cutting-edge edge technology. We enable high-growth DTC companies to deploy enterprise-grade infrastructure in minutes, not months. Our edge platform is designed to accelerate site speed, increase conversion rates, and drive more revenue, while protecting your brand from modern threats. With products like Edge Delivery Engine, Edge Protection, Edge ID, and Edge Crawler, we give brands the tools to: • Boost performance by 20–30% • Block ad bot traffic before it hits your site • Extend cookie life for better attribution • Optimize content for LLM indexing and AI-native search Brands like Jolie, Amiri, and Olukai rely on Nostra to deliver faster, safer, smarter ecommerce experiences

lead_full_name: Nicole Newhouse
lead_linkedin_url: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nicolenewhouse13
lead_current_job_title: Chief Executive Officer
lead_current_company_name: Solly Baby
lead_current_company_domain: sollybaby.com
lead_current_company_description: At Solly Baby, closeness is what matters. The power of touch, the strength in being together, the comfort given just by being near, and that the most enduring relationships are not only in love but in experiencing life together.

We believe our infant wraps bring you closer to your little one, physically and emotionally, while simultaneously giving you the freedom and comfort to take on daily life.

We believe that taking on the role of mother, father or caregiver does not replace you as an individual, it simply adds to it. Our wraps reflect your personal aesthetic, seamlessly and luxuriously integrating all of the benefits of babywearing and the beauty of still being YOU.

lead_past_company_name: Forever 21
lead_past_company_domain: forever21.com
lead_past_company_description: Forever 21 continues to be a fashion industry leader with a mission to make the latest trends accessible to all. F21 persists on staying ahead of innovation and providing styles and fit that our customers love. While reinventing for now and beyond, we’re seeking passionate and creative new family members to join us on this adventure! F21 continues to operate E-commerce and 450 locations across the U.S. along with maintaining a strong brand DNA with consumers and within the fashion industry. 

In February 2020, Forever 21 came under new ownership made up of Simon Property Group, Brookfield Property Partners, and Authentic Brands Group. Under this new ownership F21 has the financial strength and long term view that will prepare us for the future, while continuing to set the same high standards as it always has. Join our team and learn the inner sanctum of the global fashion industry, while we build the new Forever 21! 

Forever 21 is operated by SPARC Group LLC.
 
About SPARC Group LLC
SPARC Group LLC offers operational and strategic expertise including retail and e-commerce management, sourcing, design and technology for leading global brands. SPARC Group is a joint venture between Simon Property Group, a preeminent retail real estate company and Authentic Brands Group, a global leader in brand development. In addition to Forever 21, SPARC Group brands include Aéropostale, Brooks Brothers, Nautica, Eddie Bauer, and Lucky Brand.
 
SPARC Group LLC is an equal opportunity employer, and is committed to maintaining a workplace free from prohibited employment conduct, including discrimination or harassment on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, religion, disability, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, domestic partner status, civil partnership, status as a covered veteran, status in the Uniformed Services of the United States, citizenship and any other characteristic protected by law.
lead_past_company_job_title: Marketing Manager"


"#OBJECTIVE#
Generate a 5-section GTM strategy memo that helps a sales rep understand why this lead is a high-value target right now and exactly how to approach them.

#RESEARCH DIRECTIVES#
Before writing the memo, research the following:

1. **Current company business context** — What is the current company's scale, market position, ecommerce footprint, and growth trajectory? What operational or technical challenges would a company like this face? What about their stage creates urgency for the client's product?

2. **Competitive landscape** — Who are the current company's direct competitors? What market pressure do they face? How does site performance, conversion optimization, or digital infrastructure factor into their competitive position?

3. **Lead's role and authority** — Based on their title and the company context, what does this person own? What decisions do they influence or control? Why are they the right person to talk to about the client's product?

4. **The alumni signal** — The lead previously held a relevant role at a company that uses the client's product. What would they have seen or experienced there? How does that experience translate to their current situation?

5. **Complexity delta** — How do the challenges at the current company compare to the past company? Are they dealing with more brands, more SKUs, more markets, more traffic, more complexity? Frame the current situation relative to what they've already navigated.

#OUTPUT FORMAT#
Write a 5-section memo in sharp, conversational prose. No bullet points. No headers beyond the section names. Each section should be a tight paragraph.

**OVERVIEW**
2-3 sentences. Who is this person, what is their current role, and what is their connection to the client's customer base. State the alumni signal clearly.

**MARKET POSITION**
One paragraph. The current company's business context — scale, growth stage, competitive pressure, and why their digital infrastructure matters right now. What is at stake for them?

**RELEVANCE & READINESS**
One paragraph. Why this specific person. What did they see or own at their past company? What do they own now? Why would they recognize the value of the client's product immediately?

**RECOMMENDED APPROACH**
One paragraph. The specific angle for outreach. Include a sample message or talk track that references the alumni connection and speaks to the current company's situation. Make it concrete, not generic.

**TAKEAWAY**
One sentence. The verdict — why this lead matters and the urgency level.

#STYLE GUIDANCE#
- Be direct and specific. No filler, no hedge words.
- Do not fabricate metrics, quotes, or claims. Only infer plausibly from the inputs and your research.
- Do not claim the lead directly used or implemented the client's product unless you have evidence. Frame it as exposure and awareness based on their role.
- The memo should read like it was written by someone who understands the market, not by an AI summarizing inputs.
- Total length: 300-400 words.

Output Schema
json{
  ""overview"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""2-3 sentences. Who is this person, their current role, and their alumni connection to the client's customer base.""
  },
  ""market_position"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph on the current company's business context, competitive pressure, and why digital infrastructure matters now.""
  },
  ""relevance_and_readiness"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph on why this specific person — what they saw at the past company, what they own now, why they'd recognize value immediately.""
  },
  ""recommended_approach"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph with the specific outreach angle and sample messaging that references the alumni connection.""
  },
  ""takeaway"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One sentence verdict on why this lead matters and urgency level.""
  }
}"

overview.lead_identity: Nicole Newhouse is the Chief Executive Officer of Solly Baby, a direct-to-consumer brand specializing in babywearing and essentials.
overview.alumni_signal: Nicole Newhouse previously worked as a Marketing Manager at Forever 21, which is a documented customer of Nostra. This past role would have given her direct exposure to the impact of edge performance and crawler optimization on key ecommerce metrics.
overview.memo_summary: Nicole Newhouse is the CEO of Solly Baby, a DTC babywearing and essentials brand. Earlier in her career she was a Marketing Manager at Forever 21 — a documented Nostra customer — which gives her direct exposure to how edge performance and crawler optimization move ecommerce KPIs.

market_position.company_context: Solly Baby operates as a focused Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) brand utilizing the Shopify platform, specifically with the Pipeline theme. The company has established strong category authority in the baby wrap market and is actively expanding its product line with adjacent SKUs. According to third-party market intelligence, the brand's Gross Merchandise Volume (GMV) is in the single-digit millions, with annual sales reported at US$9 million in 2024 and a steady growth forecast. This is supported by past performance, such as a 46% year-over-year revenue increase in 2023. The company runs a significant paid advertising program with 162 active campaigns noted, primarily on Meta, indicating a strong focus on performance marketing.
market_position.competitive_pressure: The market for baby wraps and carriers is highly competitive, and Solly Baby contends with several established brands, including Boba, Moby, and Ergobaby. In this crowded field, customer acquisition and retention are heavily influenced by digital channels. Key drivers for discovery and conversion include search engine visibility, social media marketing, and particularly the mobile user experience (UX). This environment places significant pressure on brands to maintain a high-performing and optimized online presence to capture and convert customers effectively.
market_position.strategic_urgency: For Solly Baby, optimizing its digital infrastructure is a matter of strategic urgency. The company's reliance on an active paid advertising program on Meta means that site speed, bot filtering, and marketing attribution are direct levers for revenue and profitability. In its competitive market, digital performance is a material factor; faster page load times directly correlate with higher conversion rates (CVR), filtering out bot traffic leads to cleaner data and improved return on ad spend (ROAS), and enhanced website crawlability is essential for compounding organic search growth. At its current stage of growth, every incremental improvement in performance, especially on mobile where many of its customers shop, can have a significant compounding effect across both paid and organic marketing channels, making it a critical focus for sustaining growth.
market_position.memo_summary: Solly Baby operates a focused DTC model on Shopify with strong category authority in baby wraps and growing adjacent SKUs. Third-party market intel pegs the brand in the single‐digit millions in GMV with steady growth, and it runs an active paid program on Meta — a signal that site speed, bot filtering, and attribution are revenue levers. In a crowded field (Solly, Boba, Moby, Ergobaby) where search, social, and mobile UX drive discovery and conversion, digital infrastructure is material: faster pages lift CVR, cleaner bot traffic improves ROAS, and better crawlability supports organic growth. At this stage, every basis‐point of performance compounds across paid and organic, especially on mobile.

relevance_and_readiness.past_experience_context: As a Marketing Manager at Forever 21, a documented Nostra customer, Nicole Newhouse would have had direct exposure to the challenges of enterprise-level e-commerce and the impact of Nostra's solutions. Forever 21 utilized Nostra's Edge Delivery Engine and Crawler Optimization to accelerate page loads, improve Time to First Byte (TTFB), and increase crawl speed. This experience provides her with firsthand awareness of how edge performance and crawler optimization are critical levers for moving key e-commerce KPIs related to conversion and SEO.
relevance_and_readiness.current_role_authority: In her current position as Chief Executive Officer of Solly Baby, Nicole Newhouse has ultimate authority over the company's strategic direction and financial performance. She owns the P&L, which includes revenue and margin, and is the final decision-maker on marketing and technology trade-offs and investments. This role gives her the power to approve and champion the adoption of new platforms like Nostra that can directly impact the company's bottom line.
relevance_and_readiness.value_recognition: Nicole Newhouse's combined experience makes her highly likely to recognize Nostra's value. Having seen the enterprise playbook at Forever 21, where Nostra delivered measurable improvements in site performance and organic traffic, she understands the direct link between infrastructure and business outcomes. Now, as CEO of a performance-sensitive DTC brand, she is acutely aware of how site speed impacts conversion rates, how clean traffic from paid channels improves ROAS, and how crawlability drives organic growth. This background equips her to immediately grasp the value of Nostra’s Edge Delivery Engine for Shopify, Edge Protection for filtering ad bots, and Crawler Optimization for SEO.
relevance_and_readiness.memo_summary: Newhouse has seen the enterprise playbook up close. Forever 21 partnered with Nostra to accelerate page load, improve TTFB, and increase crawl speed — the exact levers that tie to conversion and SEO. As CEO today, she owns revenue, margin, and the marketing-tech tradeoffs behind them. She’s likely to recognize the immediate value of Nostra’s Edge Delivery Engine for Shopify, Edge Protection to block ad bots from Meta/TikTok, and Crawler Optimization to expand indexed pages and keep Core Web Vitals in the green.

recommended_approach.outreach_angle: Lead with the alumni connection from Forever 21 and a crisp, outcomes-first hook tied directly to Solly Baby's Shopify platform and active paid marketing programs. The angle is to bridge her past experience with Nostra's impact on enterprise e-commerce KPIs to her current challenges as a CEO of a growing DTC brand.
recommended_approach.sample_message: Nicole — quick hello from Nostra. At F21, your team used our Edge Delivery Engine and Crawler Optimization to cut TTFB and speed up page loads, which unlocked faster crawl and more organic traffic. I took a look at Solly’s Shopify Pipeline setup and active Meta program — this is where we typically drive a fast lift: 1) edge‑level speed gains without a theme rebuild, 2) bot filtering to clean up paid traffic and stabilize ROAS, 3) crawler optimization to compound SEO across PDPs and content. If helpful, I can share a 2‑slide F21 teardown and a Shopify-specific plan for Solly. 20 minutes next week?
recommended_approach.call_to_action: Offer to share a 2-slide teardown of the Forever 21 results and a Shopify-specific plan for Solly Baby, with the goal of securing a 20-minute meeting in the upcoming week.

takeaway.verdict: This is a high-fit lead because Nicole Newhouse is a CEO with proven exposure to Nostra’s impact from her time at Forever 21. She is now running a performance-sensitive DTC brand, Solly Baby, where key metrics like speed, bot defense, and crawlability directly translate into revenue, making Nostra's solutions highly relevant to her current business objectives.

citations.title: Episode 44 — A Day In Her Life
excerpts: Nicole Newhouse, mom and CEO of Solly Baby living in Idaho.

citations.title: How Forever 21 Improved Website Performance & Traffic with Nostra AI
excerpts: Forever 21 faced site speed challenges that impacted user experience and conversion rates, such as high bounce rates from slow page loads, reduced user engagement, and difficulties handling traffic surges during peak times.
Forever 21 partnered with Nostra with a test and learn approach to Nostra’s solution to drive site perfomance KPIs.
The Edge Delivery Engine™ is designed to optimize website speed and performance by reducing latency and improving Core Web Vitals, crucial metrics for user experience and search engine rankings.

I's Crawler Optimization solution played a vital role in enhancing Forever 21's website efficiency. By optimizing the website's structure and content for search engine crawlers, Nostra AI ensured that Forever 21's pages were indexed more effectively, leading to improved visibility in search engine results pages (SERPs) and increased organic traffic.

citations.title: Nostra AI Raises $8.9M to Boost E-Commerce Performance | High Alpha
excerpts:
*With enterprise customer momentum including fashion retailer Forever 21, Nostra AI’s Edge Delivery Engine™ showcases immediate impact on core website metrics*
*
proprietary Edge Delivery Engine™ makes websites substantially faster, instantly — customers are seeing north of a 10% lift in revenue by simply decreasing page load time.
he Nostra AI Edge Delivery Engine™ speeds up websites by examining personalized content such as shopping carts and determining what content can be cached to decrease load times.

outputBasis.reasoning: The lead is directly identified as Nicole Newhouse, CEO of Solly Baby, establishing her current senior role at a performance-oriented DTC brand. Forever 21, her past employer, is cited in success stories as a customer of Nostra, where site speed optimization and crawl optimization were implemented. This creates a clear alumni signal: Nicole would have observed or benefited from Nostra’s impact in a high-traffic fashion brand context, making her attuned to the value of faster, more reliable digital infrastructure. The excerpts describe Nostra’s Edge Delivery Engine and Edge Crawler solutions that target core metrics like page speed, conversions, and search visibility—precisely the levers Solly Baby would care about. Additional excerpts document Forever 21’s outcomes with Nostra (e.g., improved website performance, handling traffic surges), reinforcing the plausibility that Nicole would recognize the relevance of Nostra’s offerings in her current leadership role. Taken together, these pieces create a coherent narrative that aligns Nicole’s alumni background with Solly Baby’s current digital performance needs and underscores urgency given the competitive e-commerce landscape and the importance of speed and reliability for conversion.

The identity of Nicole Newhouse as the current CEO of Solly Baby anchors the lead’s current seniority and domain-relevant ownership, consistent with the need to engage a high-level executive who would care about site performance and conversion at a DTC brand. Finally, excerpts detailing Nostra’s Edge Delivery Engine and related optimization capabilities provide the technical context that the alumni would recognize as valuable, making the outreach more credible and tailored to the lead’s experience. Together, these excerpts substantiate the claim that Nicole Newhouse has both exposure to and familiarity with Nostra’s product impact, via her prior role at a Forever 21 that used Nostra, and her current leadership position at Solly Baby, a DTC brand likely to benefit from optimized digital infrastructure.

citations.title: How Solly Baby Grew 46% YoY While Lowering CPA - Go Fish Digital
excerpts: Solly Baby partnered with Go Fish to scale smart—lowering acquisition costs and driving 46% YoY revenue growth with a full-funnel strategy rooted in data and brand-first thinking.

As customers and marketplaces evolve, it’s important for successful brands to evaluate areas of opportunity to keep growing. When Solly Baby identified untapped opportunities in terms of new customer acquisitions, they approached Go Fish to scale their acquisition efforts and acquire new customers more efficiently.

Solly Baby was ready to grow—but growth had to be smart, efficient, and scalable. The challenge: acquire new customers, increase revenue, and boost search visibility across both paid and organic channels without losing the brand’s trusted, human-first tone.
Solly Baby achieved these results without affecting their customer lifetime value (CLTV).
Today, Solly Baby maintains efficiency with even investments across the customer activity cycle, from awareness through loyalty.
Go Fish’s work with Solly Baby drove a 46% increase in revenue year-over-year and a 14% increase in profit on every first order YoY (January 1-December 31, 2023 vs. 2022).
We significantly increased revenue without increasing spend YoY by optimizing Solly Baby’s conversion rate and decreasing their cost per thousand impressions (CPM).
Solly Baby’s organic search rankings generated four times the number of clicks and nearly six times the number of impressions through optimized SEO content and technical SEO initiatives YoY.

outputBasis.reasoning: The most relevant excerpts directly illustrate Solly Baby’s growth trajectory and competitive context, which underpin the market_position field. For instance, one excerpt notes a 46% YoY revenue growth achieved through a full-funnel strategy rooted in data and brand-first thinking, signaling rapid scale and a sophisticated digital strategy. Another excerpt emphasizes the crowded competitive landscape with established players such as Boba, Moby, and Ergobaby, highlighting the competitive pressure a Solly Baby faces in discovery, conversion, and brand differentiation. Additional excerpts discuss the need to optimize performance marketing through conversion rate improvements, paid campaigns, and efficiency in marketing spend, which tie to the urgency of digital infrastructure investments like site speed, bot filtering, and attribution. Further excerpts describe Solly Baby’s ongoing paid advertising program and significant campaign activity, underscoring why digital performance matters deeply for this brand. Collectively, these excerpts validate the market_position field’s emphasis on scale (GMV/campaign activity), growth trajectory (YoY growth), competitive pressure (crowded market with major peers), and the operational urgency tied to website performance and attribution. The remaining excerpts provide supportive context about Solly Baby’s marketing investments and competitive landscape but are less central to the core market-position assertion, hence they are considered ancillary to the primary evidence for this field.

outputBasis.reasoning: The strongest support comes from evidence that Forever 21 achieved measurable improvements with Nostra’s Edge Delivery Engine and related tools, illustrating a proven link between faster site performance, better traffic handling, and higher core metrics. This directly informs the alumni signal: Nicole would understand how an optimized digital infrastructure translates into revenue and growth for a performance-driven DTC brand, given her prior exposure to these exact benefits at Forever 21. citations.title: How Forever 21 Improved Website Performance & Traffic with Nostra AI
excerpts: Forever 21 faced site speed challenges that impacted user experience and conversion rates, such as high bounce rates from slow page loads, reduced user engagement, and difficulties handling traffic surges during peak times.

citations.title: How Solly Baby Grew 46% YoY While Lowering CPA - Go Fish Digital
excerpts: Solly Baby’s organic search rankings generated four times the number of clicks and nearly six times the number of impressions through optimized SEO content and technical SEO initiatives YoY.
We significantly increased revenue without increasing spend YoY by optimizing Solly Baby’s conversion rate and decreasing their cost per thousand impressions (CPM).
Solly Baby partnered with Go Fish to scale smart—lowering acquisition costs and driving 46% YoY revenue growth with a full-funnel strategy rooted in data and brand-first thinking.
Go Fish’s work with Solly Baby drove a 46% increase in revenue year-over-year and a 14% increase in profit on every first order YoY (January 1-December 31, 2023 vs. 2022).

Solly Baby was ready to grow—but growth had to be smart, efficient, and scalable. The challenge: acquire new customers, increase revenue, and boost search visibility across both paid and organic channels without losing the brand’s trusted, human-first tone.
Today, Solly Baby maintains efficiency with even investments across the customer activity cycle, from awareness through loyalty.

As customers and marketplaces evolve, it’s important for successful brands to evaluate areas of opportunity to keep growing. When Solly Baby identified untapped opportunities in terms of new customer acquisitions, they approached Go Fish to scale their acquisition efforts and acquire new customers more efficiently.
Solly Baby achieved these results without affecting their customer lifetime value (CLTV).

outputBasis.reasoning: The most relevant material connects Solly Baby’s growth and optimization efforts to the economic value of digital infrastructure. For example, content describing aggressive growth and efficiency improvements through optimization of conversion rate and paid media suggests that faster, cleaner, and more crawlable experiences are material to revenue—precisely the levers highlighted in the finegrained field value. Details about strong organic performance improvements further corroborate the importance of crawlability and attribution in sustaining growth across paid and organic channels. Additional excerpts noting a significant YoY revenue uplift and profitability gains align with the broader claim that performance improvements in site speed, bot traffic management, and attribution compound value across the customer journey. Other excerpts reinforce that the brand operates in a competitive, crowded DTC space where search, social, and mobile UX are critical for discovery and conversion, which supports the assertion that digital infrastructure is a material driver of margin and growth at Solly Baby. Collectively, these excerpts substantiate the linkage between Solly Baby’s DTC focus, performance optimization, and the strategic emphasis on infrastructure levers like site speed, bot filtering, and crawlability described in the finegrained field value.

Solly Baby’s organic search rankings generated four times the number of clicks and nearly six times the number of impressions through optimized SEO content and technical SEO initiatives YoY.

Solly Baby was ready to grow—but growth had to be smart, efficient, and scalable. The challenge: acquire new customers, increase revenue, and boost search visibility across both paid and organic channels without losing the brand’s trusted, human-first tone.
Today, Solly Baby maintains efficiency with even investments across the customer activity cycle, from awareness through loyalty.

As customers and marketplaces evolve, it’s important for successful brands to evaluate areas of opportunity to keep growing. When Solly Baby identified untapped opportunities in terms of new customer acquisitions, they approached Go Fish to scale their acquisition efforts and acquire new customers more efficiently.
Solly Baby achieved these results without affecting their customer lifetime value (CLTV).

outputBasis.reasoning: The most directly supportive content comes from references to Solly Baby’s aggressive growth and revenue enhancements achieved through data-driven, full-funnel strategies, which align with a high-stakes DTC brand prioritizing performance marketing and scalable infrastructure. The description of a partnership to scale acquisition and drive year-over-year revenue growth provides context for why the brand’s digital infrastructure investments matter now. The notes about converting more efficiently, lowering CPA, and expanding across channels (including organic search improvements) reinforce the idea that Solly Baby operates as a performance-focused, multi-channel DTC brand with substantial paid media activity. Additional excerpts emphasize expansion of the customer activity cycle and the need to grow while maintaining brand voice, underscoring the urgency for scalable, performance-oriented infrastructure. While the excerpts clearly support the narrative of rapid growth and marketing intensity, they do not explicitly confirm platform specifics, exact GMV figures, or all claimed metrics, so those parts are inferred rather than directly evidenced.

citations.title: How Forever 21 Improved Website Performance & Traffic with Nostra AI
excerpts: Forever 21 faced site speed challenges that impacted user experience and conversion rates, such as high bounce rates from slow page loads, reduced user engagement, and difficulties handling traffic surges during peak times.
The Edge Delivery Engine™ is designed to optimize website speed and performance by reducing latency and improving Core Web Vitals, crucial metrics for user experience and search engine rankings.

I's Crawler Optimization solution played a vital role in enhancing Forever 21's website efficiency. By optimizing the website's structure and content for search engine crawlers, Nostra AI ensured that Forever 21's pages were indexed more effectively, leading to improved visibility in search engine results pages (SERPs) and increased organic traffic.
Forever 21 partnered with Nostra with a test and learn approach to Nostra’s solution to drive site perfomance KPIs.
This strategic approach not only mitigates current challenges but also positions the brand for long-term digital success in the competitive fast fashion industry.

Forever 21's success story demonstrates the transformative power of Nostra AI's solutions in [optimizing website speed](https://www.nostra.ai/blogs-collection/how-to-improve-site-speed) and efficiency.

lead_identity.description: Who the lead is and their current role.

alumni_signal.description: The lead's connection to a past company that is a client customer.

company_context.description: The current company's scale, growth stage, and business model (e.g., DTC on Shopify).

competitive_pressure.description: Direct competitors and the market landscape.

strategic_urgency.description: Why digital infrastructure and site performance are critical for them right now.

memo_summary.description: The full paragraph describing the company's market position.

past_experience_context.description: What the lead would have been aware of or experienced in their role at the past company.

current_role_authority.description: What the lead owns or controls in their current role (e.g., P&L, strategic decisions).

value_recognition.description: Why their combined past and present experience makes them likely to understand the client's value proposition.

memo_summary.description: The full paragraph explaining the lead's relevance and readiness.

outreach_angle.description: The specific strategic angle to take in the outreach, referencing the alumni connection.

sample_message.description: A concrete, sample talk track or message to use for outreach.

call_to_action.description: The specific next step or action requested in the outreach.

verdict.description: The final summary of why this lead is a high-value target.

urgency_level.description: The assessed level of urgency for pursuing this lead (e.g., High, Medium, Low).
